_meta.description: 2026 US state supplemental-wage (bonus/commission) withholding methods + rates for all 50 states and DC, plus the federal flat-22%/37% rule. Consumed by the bonus-tax calculator cluster. Kept SEPARATE from tax-data-2026.json because supplemental rates change on a different cadence and some (e.g. NC 4.09% supp vs 3.99% income) deliberately differ from the state income-tax rate.

methods.none: No state income tax on wages -> 0% state withholding on a bonus.
methods.flat: State (or the standard payroll-industry practice) applies a flat supplemental rate as bonus x rate when the bonus is paid separately.
methods.regular: No separate supplemental rate; withhold as if the bonus were ordinary wages (aggregate/regular method). The calculator computes this with the existing paycheck engine: stateTax(regular+bonus) - stateTax(regular).
methods.special: Non-simple formula: California (two flat rates by payment type), Vermont (30% of the FEDERAL withholding, not of the bonus), Wisconsin (four-band graduated by annual gross).

_meta.verificationGate: Every flat/special supplemental rate that originally rested on a single payroll-industry aggregator (Patriot Software) was put through a per-state confirmation gate on 2026-07-11. Outcome: all 18 flagged rates were independently confirmed against a state Department of Revenue guide and/or a second independent payroll provider (Deel/OnPay/APA/EY). One correction resulted: Ohio's 2025 rate of 3.5% dropped to 2.75% effective 1/1/2026 (flat-tax alignment, Ohio Admin. Rule 5703-7-10) and is stored corrected. No rate ships single-sourced. Fields: verified=true means independently corroborated; singleSourced=true (none at present) would flag a rate resting on the aggregator alone, which the page discloses as 'verify with your state's Department of Revenue.'

sources.federal: IRS Publication 15 (Circular E), 2026, Supplemental Wages: https://www.irs.gov/publications/p15
sources.patriot: Patriot Software, 2026 Supplemental Tax Rates by State (original aggregator, since corroborated): https://www.patriotsoftware.com/blog/payroll/supplemental-tax-rates-by-state/
sources.ey2026: EY Tax News, 2026 state supplemental flat tax and highest income-tax withholding rates: https://taxnews.ey.com/news/2026-0153-2026-state-supplemental-flat-tax-and-highest-income-tax-withholding-rates-with-hyperlinks-to-the-latest-withholding-tables-instructions
sources.deel: Deel, Supplemental Tax Rates by State (independent payroll provider): https://www.deel.com/blog/supplemental-tax-rates-by-state/
sources.repoTaxData: Per-state income-tax status/type/rates from src/data/tax-data-2026.json (_meta.lastSourced 2026-06-16, sourced to each state DOR).

federal._source: IRS Pub 15 (2026): flat 22% on supplemental wages up to $1,000,000/yr; mandatory 37% on the portion above $1,000,000. TCJA rates made permanent by P.L. 119-21 (OBBBA), so 22%/37% carry into 2026 unchanged.

alabama.name: Alabama
alabama.source: Alabama DOR employer withholding tables (rev. Jan 2026); corroborated by Deel/OnPay

arizona.name: Arizona
arizona.note: No separate supplemental rate; aggregate method. Arizona income tax is a flat 2.5%, so a separately paid bonus is effectively withheld near 2.5%.

arkansas.name: Arkansas
arkansas.source: Arkansas DFA withholding instructions (3.9% since 7/1/2024); corroborated by OnPay

california.name: California
california.note: 10.23% on bonuses and stock options; 6.6% on other supplemental wages. California SDI (1.3%, no wage cap in 2026) is also withheld but is not an income tax.

colorado.name: Colorado
colorado.note: No separate supplemental rate; aggregate method. Colorado income tax is a flat 4.4%.

connecticut.name: Connecticut
connecticut.note: No separate supplemental rate; withhold using the graduated income-tax tables (aggregate method).

delaware.name: Delaware
delaware.note: No separate flat supplemental rate for a regular bonus; aggregate method. (Delaware suggests a 5.0% rate only for deferred compensation.)

district-of-columbia.name: District of Columbia
district-of-columbia.note: No separate supplemental rate; withhold using the graduated income-tax tables (aggregate method).

georgia.name: Georgia
georgia.note: No separate supplemental rate; aggregate method. Georgia income tax is a flat 4.99% for 2026 (verified against HB 463; further trigger-based cuts toward 3.99% are future).

hawaii.name: Hawaii
hawaii.note: No separate supplemental rate; withhold using the graduated income-tax tables (aggregate method).

idaho.name: Idaho
idaho.source: Idaho State Tax Commission (optional flat 5.3%, HB 40); corroborated by Deel
idaho.note: Optional flat method when the bonus is paid separately; otherwise aggregate. Matches Idaho's top income rate of 5.3%.

illinois.name: Illinois
illinois.note: No separate supplemental rate; aggregate method. Illinois income tax is a flat 4.95%.

indiana.name: Indiana
indiana.note: No separate supplemental rate; aggregate method. Indiana income tax is a flat 2.95% (county income taxes apply on top and are not modeled).

iowa.name: Iowa
iowa.source: Iowa DOR withholding tax information (flat 3.8%, ties to 2026 flat income tax); corroborated by Netchex
iowa.note: Applied when the bonus is paid separately.

kansas.name: Kansas
kansas.source: Kansas DOR KW-100 withholding guide; corroborated by OnPay/Netchex
kansas.note: Applied when the bonus is paid separately.

kentucky.name: Kentucky
kentucky.note: No separate supplemental rate; aggregate method. Kentucky income tax is a flat 3.5%.

louisiana.name: Louisiana
louisiana.note: No separate supplemental rate; aggregate method. Louisiana income tax is a flat 3.0%.

maine.name: Maine
maine.source: Maine Revenue Services 2026 withholding (flat 5% separate pay, eff. 1/1/2026, via APA/PayrollOrg); corroborated by Deel
maine.note: Optional flat method if the bonus is paid separately; otherwise aggregate.

maryland.name: Maryland
maryland.note: No separate flat supplemental rate; aggregate method. County piggyback income taxes apply on top and are not modeled.

massachusetts.name: Massachusetts
massachusetts.note: No separate supplemental rate; aggregate method. Massachusetts is 5% flat with a 4% surtax on income over $1M.

michigan.name: Michigan
michigan.note: No separate supplemental rate; aggregate method. Michigan income tax is a flat 4.25% (some city income taxes apply on top and are not modeled).

mississippi.name: Mississippi
mississippi.note: No separate supplemental rate; aggregate method. Mississippi taxes income above an exemption at roughly 4.0%.

missouri.name: Missouri
missouri.source: Missouri DOR Employer's Tax Guide Form 4282 (rev. 03-2026): flat 4.7%; corroborated by OnPay

montana.name: Montana
montana.source: Montana DOR Publication 1 (2026): 'a flat 5 percent of the supplemental wage'

nebraska.name: Nebraska
nebraska.source: Nebraska DOR Circular EN (2026): flat 3.5%; corroborated by OnPay

new-hampshire.name: New Hampshire
new-hampshire.note: No tax on wage income (the interest/dividends tax has been repealed); 0% on a bonus.

new-jersey.name: New Jersey
new-jersey.note: No separate flat supplemental rate; withhold using the graduated income-tax tables (aggregate method).

new-mexico.name: New Mexico
new-mexico.source: New Mexico top marginal rate 5.9% applied to separately-paid supplemental wages (Deel; no distinct state flat-supplemental method published)
new-mexico.note: New Mexico does not publish a dedicated separate-payment flat rate; 5.9% (the top marginal rate) is the rate payroll providers apply to a separately paid bonus.

new-york.name: New York
new-york.localNote: New York City adds a 4.25% supplemental rate and Yonkers adds 1.61135% (resident) / 0.5% (non-resident) on top of the 11.7% state rate. Those local rates are not included in the headline figure.

north-carolina.name: North Carolina
north-carolina.source: North Carolina DOR NC-30 (2026): 'withhold a flat 4.09 percent' (= 3.99% income rate + 0.1%)
north-carolina.note: North Carolina's 4.09% supplemental withholding rate is deliberately DISTINCT from its 3.99% flat income-tax rate - both confirmed against NC-30 (2026).

north-dakota.name: North Dakota
north-dakota.source: North Dakota DOR 2026 income tax withholding booklet: 'Multiply the supplemental wages by 1.50% (.0150)'

ohio.name: Ohio
ohio.source: Ohio Administrative Rule 5703-7-10 / ODT: supplemental rate dropped 3.5% -> 2.75% eff. 1/1/2026 (flat-tax alignment); corroborated by OnPay/Deel/PaycheckCity 2026
ohio.note: Ohio's supplemental withholding rate fell to 2.75% for 2026 to match its new flat income-tax rate (was 3.5% in 2025).

oklahoma.name: Oklahoma
oklahoma.source: Oklahoma top marginal rate 4.5% (2026, was 4.75%) applied to separately-paid supplemental wages (OW-2 / Deel)
oklahoma.note: Oklahoma does not publish a dedicated separate-payment flat rate; 4.5% (the top marginal rate) is the rate payroll providers apply to a separately paid bonus.

pennsylvania.name: Pennsylvania
pennsylvania.note: No separate supplemental rate; aggregate method. Pennsylvania income tax is a flat 3.07% with no standard deduction, so a separately paid bonus is effectively withheld at 3.07% (local earned-income taxes apply on top and are not modeled).

rhode-island.name: Rhode Island
rhode-island.source: Rhode Island DOR 2026 Withholding Tax Booklet: 5.99%; corroborated by Deel

south-carolina.name: South Carolina
south-carolina.note: No published flat supplemental rate; aggregate method (certain payments are withheld at the top marginal rate).

tennessee.name: Tennessee
tennessee.note: No state income tax on wages.

utah.name: Utah
utah.note: No separate supplemental rate; aggregate method. Utah income tax is a flat 4.5%.

vermont.name: Vermont
vermont.source: Vermont Dept. of Taxes GB-1210 (2026): 30% of federal withholding; corroborated by Deel + NFC/USDA 2026 bulletin
vermont.note: Vermont supplemental withholding is 30% of the FEDERAL income tax withheld on the bonus (not a percent of the bonus). 6% applies to nonqualified deferred comp.

virginia.name: Virginia
virginia.source: Virginia Tax employer withholding instructions: flat 5.75%; corroborated by Deel
virginia.note: Optional flat method, or aggregate if income tax was already withheld from regular wages.

washington.name: Washington
washington.note: No tax on wage income (the 7% tax applies only to large long-term capital gains); 0% on a bonus.

west-virginia.name: West Virginia
west-virginia.note: No separate supplemental rate; withhold using the graduated income-tax tables (aggregate method).

wisconsin.name: Wisconsin
wisconsin.source: Wisconsin DOR Publication W-166 (2026): four-band graduated supplemental rate (all bands + thresholds verified verbatim)
